w:r.w:t: KaamSetu is a trust-first blue-collar job matching platform that connects verified workers (drivers, security guards, cooks, helpers, domestic staff) with employers (households, logistics companies, factories, hotels). The core differentiator is continuous, automated background re-verification through Authbridge — the platform does not merely verify workers once at onboarding but monitors them periodically, notifying employers and blocking profiles the moment a new criminal case is detected.
The name KaamSetu means 'work bridge' in Hindi, reflecting the platform's mission to build a safe, trusted bridge between the workforce and employers across India.
No reliable way to verify blue-collar workers before hiring — most hiring is word-of-mouth
No visibility into criminal history or post-hire case registrations
High fraud risk: fake documents, misrepresented experience
Existing platforms (WorkIndia, Apna) list profiles but do not verify or re-check them
Informal, opaque hiring process with no digital paper trail or offer letter
No portable, trusted digital identity that demonstrates their track record
Exploitative intermediaries (agents) take cuts without adding safety
Domestic workers, drivers, and guards are hired with zero background verification in 95% of cases
No platform offers ongoing re-verification after hire — this is the untapped moat
Vision: To become India's most trusted source of verified blue-collar talent — making safe hiring the default, not the exception.
Launch Worker app (Kaamgar) and Employer app on Android
Onboard 10,000 verified workers across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Bangalore
Complete Authbridge integration for criminal background checks
Complete Surepass DigiLocker Aadhaar integration for instant KYC
Launch digital offer letter with eSign (Aadhaar OTP based)
Launch referral program for worker acquisition
Payroll and compliance management for employers
Insurance and financial products for workers
Hindi, Bengali, Tamil, Telugu, Marathi, Kannada
Find steady, fair-pay employment with dignity
No trusted proof of clean background; agents exploit them
Urban household hiring driver, cook, guard, housekeeper
Safety — who is entering my home?
Moderate; uses Swiggy, Zomato, Urban Company
Logistics company, hotel, hospital, factory, security agency
API access, bulk hiring, offer letter automation
Language selection (Hindi, English, 8 regional). Single tap.
Phone number entry, OTP verification via SMS. 6-digit OTP, 30s resend timer.
Full name (Aadhaar-matched), DOB, gender, father's name, photo upload, education level chips
Job type chips (Driver, Guard, Cook, Helper, Peon…), specialisation, experience chips, licence number
Last 2 employers: name, duration, role, reference contact. Optional second employer.
GPS auto-detect, living address, pincode, preferred job cities (chips), live-in toggle, pan-India toggle
Aadhaar via DigiLocker/Surepass API, PAN entry, selfie capture (face match), Authbridge criminal check initiation, address verification
Profile score, verification badges, open-to-work toggle, matched job alerts, distance-tagged job cards
Case details, block reason, employer notification status, upload court clearance, contact support
Company name, entity type chips, GST, PAN, TAN, CIN, registered address, HR contact name and mobile
GST/PAN/TAN verification status, manual review pending, activation timeline
Active requirements count, matched count, hired count, requirement cards, post new button
Job type, number of openings, salary range, location, start date, pan-India toggle, live-in toggle, minimum experience
Verified worker cards sorted by distance, filter chips (nearby/pan-india/live-in), match count, scroll list
Photo, skills, work history, all verification badges, distance, send interest, shortlist actions
Worker name, case type, district, date detected, platform action taken (blocked), employer advised action, mark action taken
Offer details, eSign status, auto re-check schedule notice, download offer letter PDF
Platform KPIs, urgent alerts count, blocked today, KYC pending, quick action buttons
Pending profiles table, oldest first, review button per profile, approve/reject actions
Nightly re-check results, new cases flagged, one-click block and notify employers, export report
Requirement details, match score breakdown per worker, score factors (location, skills, experience, verification, live-in)
Blocked worker details, documents submitted checklist, approve/reject, auto-notify employers on reinstatement
Hires per month, avg time to hire, verification pass rate, blocked profiles, top cities bar chart
Single codebase, Android-first, EAS build for APK
Predictable state, built-in caching for API calls
Fast to build, JSON-native, wide ecosystem
ACID compliance critical for KYC and verification data
OTP TTL, job queue for nightly re-checks, session cache
Nightly Authbridge re-check runs for all active workers
Aadhaar XML, selfies, court clearance documents
Android push for case alerts, job matches
Criminal record, FIR history, court records pan-India
UPI, card, net banking; subscription and per-hire billing
India data residency for DPDP Act compliance
Returns: Full name, DOB, gender, masked Aadhaar, profile photo (base64), full address (structured), father name, XML URL
Use cases: Worker onboarding KYC, address auto-population, name match with PAN
Use cases: Initial criminal check on onboarding, nightly re-verification of all active workers
Checks performed: FIR history (pan-India), court records, police verification, watchlist screening, adverse media
Integration pattern: Webhook-based — Authbridge calls back to our endpoint when check is complete
Validates employer GST number against official GSTN database
Validates PAN for both workers and employers, confirms name and DOB match
Primary database: PostgreSQL. All tables use UUID primary keys. Sensitive fields encrypted at rest using AES-256. All timestamps in UTC.
Auth table: mobile, OTP hash, JWT tokens, role (worker/employer/admin)
Worker profile: personal details, occupation, preferences, profile score
Many-to-many: workers to skills (driver, cook, guard, etc.)
Previous employers, duration, role, reference contact
Current address, preferred cities, live-in flag, pan-India flag
Aadhaar (masked), PAN, selfie S3 URL, driving licence, other docs
Verification status per check type, timestamp, response payload
Authbridge check ID, status, result, report URL, run timestamp
Block reason, case details, blocked by, blocked at, reinstated at
Company profile: name, entity type, GST, PAN, TAN, CIN, address
Job postings: job type, salary range, location, openings, live-in, pan-india
Auto-matched pairs: requirement_id, worker_id, match_score, score_breakdown
Confirmed hires: requirement_id, worker_id, offer details, offer_letter_url
Generated offer letter metadata, eSign status, PDF S3 URL
FCM push notification log: recipient, type, payload, delivered_at
All admin actions: who did what and when (immutable append-only)
OTP code (hashed), mobile, expires_at, attempts, used flag
Exact match = 30, related category = 15, no match = 0
Meets min requirement = 20, one year below = 10, far below = 0
If required and worker ok = 10, not required = 10 (neutral)
Worker must not be blocked (blocked_at IS NULL)
Worker must have passed latest background check (cases_found = false)
This is the core trust mechanism and primary product differentiator.
Nightly cron job (2:00 AM IST) scans all workers where next_check_due <= NOW()
Default re-check interval: 180 days from last clear check
Admin can trigger manual re-check on any worker at any time
Workers who are hired (appear in hires table) are re-checked every 90 days
Step 1: Authbridge returns flagged result via webhook.
Step 2: System immediately sets worker.kyc_status = 'blocked' and worker.blocked_at = NOW().
Step 3: Worker's profile is removed from all active match results and requirement searches.
Step 4: All employers with this worker in their shortlists or hires receive instant FCM push notification + SMS alert.
Step 5: Worker receives FCM push notification with case details and reinstatement instructions.
Step 6: Admin panel displays the new case in the Case Alert Management queue with one-click block-and-notify action.
Step 7: Audit log records the block action with timestamp, case details, and affected employer count.
Worker uploads court clearance document (PDF/image) via blocked screen
Admin reviews document in reinstatement queue
If clear: worker.kyc_status = 'fully_verified', blocked_at = NULL, next_check_due reset
All previously affected employers receive FCM notification: 'Worker Ramesh Kumar has been reinstated'
Explicit, purpose-specific consent before collecting Aadhaar or criminal history data
Consent must be in the user's chosen language (8 regional languages supported)
Data minimisation: collect only what is required for matching and verification
Aadhaar data stored encrypted, masked in all UI displays (XXXXXXXX1234 format)
User right to access: download own data from profile settings
User right to erasure: delete account wipes personal data (verification status retained in audit log for 1 year per DPDP Rules 2025)
Data retention: active user data retained while account is active; deleted after 1 year of inactivity
Data residency: all data stored in AWS ap-south-1 (India)
Breach notification: report to Data Protection Board within 72 hours of confirmed breach
Appoint Data Protection Officer (DPO) before public launch
Never store full 12-digit Aadhaar number — only masked form (XXXXXXXX1234)
Aadhaar data used only for identity verification, not profiling or marketing
No Aadhaar data shared with third parties beyond UIDAI-authorised flows
All Aadhaar API calls routed through licensed Authentication User Agency (AUA) — Surepass holds AUA licence
JWT tokens with 15-minute expiry; refresh tokens with 30-day expiry
AES-256 encryption for all documents at rest on S3
API rate limiting: 100 req/min per IP, 1000 req/min per authenticated user
OWASP Top 10 security checklist before production launch
Digital offer letter must include: parties, role, salary, start date, notice period, terms
eSign via Aadhaar OTP is legally valid under IT Act Section 3A
Platform is a marketplace, not an employer — terms of service must state this clearly
Worker registration, profile, KYC (Aadhaar + PAN + selfie)
Nightly re-verification cron job with auto-block and employer notification
Employer registration, company KYC (GST + PAN + TAN)
Worker blocked screen and reinstatement upload
Admin panel: KYC queue, case management, reinstatement
FCM push notifications for all critical events
In-app messaging between employer and worker
Worker rating by employer after 30 days
Prerequisites: Node.js 20+, Expo CLI, EAS CLI, Android SDK (optional for local)
Step 3: Configure eas.json in project root
eas build --platform android --profile preview # Debug APK
eas build --platform android --profile production # Production AAB for Play Store
Step 4: Download APK from Expo dashboard
APK link is emailed and available at expo.dev dashboard after ~10 minutes
Dockerised Node.js backend, deployed to AWS ECS (Fargate)
PostgreSQL on AWS RDS (ap-south-1), multi-AZ for production
S3 bucket in ap-south-1 with server-side encryption (SSE-S3)
API Gateway + CloudFront CDN for low-latency across India
Queue check, allow partial profile creation; show 'Verification in progress' badge; retry with exponential backoff
Surepass API rate limit hit at scale
Cache DigiLocker responses per client_id; implement request queue with Redis Bull
False positive criminal case blocks genuine worker
Admin manual review before auto-block; worker can appeal with documents; 48-hour review SLA
Consent screen before any data collection; multilingual; logged with timestamp
Database-level constraint: aadhaar_number column stores only masked format; logs scrubbed
Voice-assisted form fill, icon-based chips, local language, helpline number visible throughout
Allow household employers to register with PAN only (no GST); GST required for companies only
Follow Google policy on background check apps; clear privacy policy URL in app and Play Store listing
Average time from signup to verified profile
Key response fields used by KaamSetu:
data.aadhaar_xml_data.profile_image → uploaded to S3 as worker profile photo
Authbridge requires direct API contract — contact: communication@authbridge.com
Criminal check result delivered via webhook (HTTP POST to your endpoint)
Typical TAT: instant to 24 hours depending on check depth
Webhook payload includes: reference_id, status (CLEAR/FLAGGED), case_details array, report_url
Store full webhook payload encrypted in worker_background_checks.result_payload (JSONB)
Use Authbridge sandbox environment for development and testing
